codeReview.summary: Feature implements pattern learning module for extracting anti-patterns from rejected tasks. Build passes and 29 tests pass. However, 3 blocking issues from prior round remain unfixed.

issues.description: Confidence calculation in deduplicate_anti_patterns uses wrong denominator: frequency_map.values().sum() gives sum of ALL pattern frequencies instead of total number of rejections
issues.evidence: Line ~955: final_pattern.update_confidence(frequency_map.values().sum()); - The update_confidence function divides self.frequency by this value, which results in self.frequency/total_all_frequencies. This is incorrect because it means confidence depends on how many other patterns exist, not how frequent this pattern is relative to total rejections.

issues.description: Convention parsing uses wrong string slice index [3..] instead of [4..]
issues.evidence: Line 1145: let name = trimmed[3..colon_pos].trim().to_string(); - For a line like '- **my_convention**: description', position 3 is the first '*' character, so trimmed[3..colon_pos] would include the leading '*'. Should be trimmed[4..colon_pos] to skip the '- **' prefix.

issues.description: get_anti_patterns_for_error is an unimplemented stub returning empty vec
issues.evidence: Lines 1173-1178: pub async fn get_anti_patterns_for_error(...) -> Result<Vec<AntiPattern>, SwellError> { let _ = error_type; Ok(Vec::new()) } - The function ignores the error_type parameter and always returns an empty vector. Comment says 'For MVP, return empty' but this is a blocking issue for user testing since anti-pattern retrieval does not work.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The convention parsing logic uses markdown format '- **name**: description' but the slice index doesn't correctly extract the name portion
sharedStateObservations.evidence: crates/swell-memory/src/pattern_learning.rs:1145 - uses [3..] but should use [4..] for lines starting with '- **'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker reported following the Rust skill procedure, but the implementation shows several logic errors that suggest the skill's guidance on confidence calculation and string parsing may need more specific examples
sharedStateObservations.evidence: skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true, yet 3 blocking issues remain in core logic

summary: RE-RUN REVIEW: All 3 blocking issues from round 11 remain unfixed. (1) Confidence calculation divides by sum of all frequencies instead of total rejections. (2) Convention parsing uses wrong string slice index. (3) get_anti_patterns_for_error is an unimplemented stub. Build passes and 29 tests pass, but tests do not cover the buggy code paths. These issues must be fixed before user testing.
